title: pisum sativum var. arvense
intro: Pisum sativum var. arvense, also known as wild garden pea or field pea, is a variety of the pea plant that is native to Europe and Asia. It has been used in herbal medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antioxidant effects, as well as its ability to support digestive health. The health benefits of Pisum sativum var. arvense include reducing inflammation and improving symptoms of irritable bowel syndrome (IBS), as well as supporting the immune system and promoting overall well-being. Herbal preparations of Pisum sativum var. arvense may include teas, infusions, and tinctures, which can be used to take advantage of its medicinal properties.
